questions.q: In which year was the Poona Pact concluded?
questions.explanation: The Poona Pact was concluded in 1932.

questions.q: The Poona Pact was an agreement between Mahatma Gandhi and which leader?
questions.explanation: The pact was concluded between Gandhi and B. R. Ambedkar.

questions.q: What did the Poona Pact replace for the depressed classes?
questions.explanation: The pact replaced separate electorates with reserved seats for the depressed classes.

questions.q: Instead of separate electorates, what did the Poona Pact provide for the depressed classes?
options: Reserved seats in the general electorate
questions.explanation: It provided reserved seats for the depressed classes within the general (joint) electorate.

questions.q: The Poona Pact followed Gandhi's fast against which British decision?
questions.explanation: Gandhi undertook his fast in protest against the Communal Award's grant of separate electorates.

questions.q: In which city was the Poona Pact signed?
questions.explanation: The agreement was signed at Poona (modern Pune), giving it its name.

questions.q: Gandhi began his fast leading to the Poona Pact while imprisoned at which place?
questions.explanation: Gandhi was held at Yerwada Central Jail in Poona when he began his fast.

questions.q: The term 'depressed classes' in the Poona Pact referred to which group?
options: The communities later called Scheduled Castes
questions.explanation: The 'depressed classes' broadly referred to the communities now known as the Scheduled Castes.

questions.q: Gandhi's protest against the Communal Award took the form of what?
questions.explanation: Gandhi announced a fast unto death against separate electorates for the depressed classes.

questions.q: The Poona Pact is significant in the history of which broader cause?
options: Representation and rights of the depressed classes
questions.explanation: The pact is a landmark in debates over the political representation and rights of the depressed classes.

questions.q: On what date in 1932 was the Poona Pact signed?
questions.explanation: The Poona Pact was signed on 24 September 1932.

questions.q: The Communal Award that triggered the pact was announced by which British Prime Minister?
questions.explanation: Prime Minister Ramsay MacDonald announced the Communal Award in August 1932.

questions.q: Compared with separate electorates, the Poona Pact gave the depressed classes what change in the number of reserved seats?
options: A much larger number of reserved seats
questions.explanation: The pact increased the number of reserved seats for the depressed classes well beyond what the Communal Award offered.

questions.q: Roughly how many seats in the provincial legislatures were reserved for the depressed classes under the Poona Pact?
questions.explanation: The pact reserved about 148 seats for the depressed classes in the provincial legislatures, up from 71 under the Award.

questions.q: Under the Poona Pact, how were candidates for reserved seats to be chosen?
options: By a primary panel of depressed-class voters, then the joint electorate
questions.explanation: Depressed-class voters would select a panel by primary, after which the joint electorate elected the representative.

questions.q: Which prominent reformer and writer mediated between Gandhi and Ambedkar during the crisis?
questions.explanation: Madan Mohan Malaviya and others worked to broker the agreement as Gandhi's health declined.

questions.q: The Poona Pact also included a provision for the depressed classes in which area beyond legislative seats?
questions.explanation: The pact pledged a fair share in public services and the earmarking of funds for the education of the depressed classes.

questions.q: Gandhi began his fast against the Communal Award in which month of 1932?
questions.explanation: Gandhi began his fast unto death on 20 September 1932, days before the pact was signed.

questions.q: Why did Ambedkar ultimately agree to the Poona Pact despite his reservations?
options: Because he had always opposed separate electorates
To save Gandhi's life and avoid a backlash against the depressed classes
Because the British forced him to
questions.explanation: Ambedkar agreed largely to save Gandhi's life and to protect his community from possible reprisals if Gandhi died.

questions.q: The reserved seats under the Poona Pact were to operate within what kind of electorate?
questions.explanation: Reserved seats were placed within a joint general electorate rather than a separate one.

questions.q: The provisions of the Poona Pact were later incorporated into which statute governing India?
questions.explanation: The pact's arrangements on reserved seats fed into the communal provisions of the Government of India Act 1935.

questions.q: Ambedkar signed the pact on behalf of the depressed classes; on whose side was it primarily endorsed besides Gandhi?
options: Caste-Hindu leaders of the Congress and reform bodies
questions.explanation: Caste-Hindu leaders associated with the Congress and Hindu reform organisations endorsed the pact alongside Gandhi.

questions.q: Under the Communal Award, the depressed classes were to have both separate electorates and what additional voting right?
options: Reserved seats in the Council of State only
A double vote, also voting in the general constituency
questions.explanation: The Award gave the depressed classes separate electorates plus a vote in general constituencies for a transitional period.

questions.q: The primary-election mechanism for reserved seats under the Poona Pact was to operate for how long before review?
options: A limited number of years, after which it could be ended by mutual agreement
questions.explanation: The panel/primary system was to run for a limited period and could be terminated by mutual agreement of the communities.

questions.q: Scholars note a lasting disagreement between Gandhi and Ambedkar over what core question behind the pact?
options: Whether separate electorates would help or harm the depressed classes' unity with Hindu society
questions.explanation: Gandhi feared separate electorates would split the depressed classes from Hindu society, a view Ambedkar contested as denying them real political power.

questions.q: The Poona Pact reserved seats in the central legislature for the depressed classes amounting to what share?
options: 18 per cent of the general seats for British India
questions.explanation: The pact reserved 18 per cent of the general seats for British India in the central legislature for the depressed classes.

questions.q: After the pact, Ambedkar later expressed that he felt he had been placed in a position where he could not freely refuse, describing his consent in what terms?
options: As given under moral duress because of Gandhi's fast
As a complete victory for his cause
questions.explanation: Ambedkar later wrote that Gandhi's fast amounted to coercion, leaving him little choice but to agree to save Gandhi's life.

questions.q: Gandhi referred to members of the depressed classes by which term that Ambedkar found objectionable?
questions.explanation: Gandhi called the depressed classes 'Harijans', a term Ambedkar and many leaders later rejected as patronising.

questions.q: Following the Poona Pact, Gandhi launched a campaign and weekly publication focused on which cause?
options: The anti-untouchability Harijan movement and the journal Harijan
questions.explanation: After the pact Gandhi intensified the anti-untouchability campaign and started the weekly journal 'Harijan'.

questions.q: The system of reserved seats established by the Poona Pact prefigured which feature retained in independent India's Constitution?
options: Reservation of seats for Scheduled Castes in legislatures
questions.explanation: The principle of reserved legislative seats for the depressed classes evolved into reservation for Scheduled Castes in the Constitution.
